page: Microsoft Azure B4ms is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the burstable BS family, virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 4 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz, with a 35 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 33 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark data indicates that the B4ms is positioned in the lower performance tier for compute-heavy tasks. Both stress-ng and Geekbench single-core and multi-core CPU tests place the instance in the bottom 25 percent of tested hardware. Similarly, large-block uncached memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput are weak. However, the server demonstrates average performance in database operations, Redis SET operations achieving 5.88 million operations per second, and small-block cached memory read bandwidth. Small-model LLM inference also registers in the average tier, though performance drops significantly on larger models.

The burstable CPU allocation model of the B4ms offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads with variable CPU utilization. Because the CPU resources are burstable, the instance is not designed for sustained high-compute workloads, which represents a key tradeoff. It is suitable for low-traffic web servers, small database operations, development environments, and lightweight background tasks that require moderate memory density but experience intermittent CPU demand.
description: Microsoft Azure B4ms is an x86_64 burstable server from the BS family, featuring 4 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, 33 GB of local storage, and supports Premium Storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show weak performance in compute-intensive tasks, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores in the bottom 25 percent. However, it achieves average results in database operations, Redis SET operations at 5.88 million operations per second, and small-block cached memory reads. The burstable CPU allocation provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads with fluctuating demand. This server is suitable for development environments, small databases, and low-traffic web applications that do not require sustained CPU performance.
